articles.title: NYSE Arca files for Cardano ETF on behalf of Grayscale as crypto proposals gain momentum
articles.description: A subsidiary of the New York Stock Exchange, on behalf of Grayscale, filed to create a spot Cardano exchange-traded fund.

articles.title: North Carolina becomes 20th US state to introduce legislation for a Bitcoin reserve
articles.description: North Carolina lawmakers have introduced a bill allowing the state treasurer to invest up to 10% in Bitcoin (BTC) and other qualifying digital assets. House Bill 92, sponsored by Representative Destin Hall and Representatives Mark Brody and Steve Ross, qualifies any crypto with a market cap of over $750 billion during the 12 months preceding […] The post North Carolina becomes 20th US state to...

articles.title: 'Ethereum Stole the Show': ETH Funds Trump Bitcoin ETF Gains
articles.description: Ethereum-related funds outpaced Bitcoin for the first time this year after investors last week saw a buying opportunity for the coin.

articles.title: Ethereum security platform Drosera raises $3.25 million in funding, brings total funding to $4.75 million
articles.description: Drosera builds out "traps," or smart contracts that can detect and cancel out threats on the Ethereum network.

articles.title: Over $287M in SOL Removed from Circulation via Pump.fun’s Graduated Token Mechanism
articles.description: More than 74. 6% of the liquidity pools moved to Raydium are inactive, with more than 1. 38 million SOL locked in them.

articles.title: Trump’s World Liberty Fi buys more MOVE, stakes Ethereum (ETH) on chain
articles.description: World Liberty Financial, the Trump family DeFi fund, once again bought 803K MOVE, potentially adding hype to the Movement project. The fund divested some of its previous holdings in a portfolio reorganization.

articles.title: Hong Kong Approves BTC & ETH for Immigration Assets
articles.description: The Hong Kong authorities recognize BTC and Ethereum as valid ways to confirm assets within investment immigration protocols. The HK$30 million ($3.8 million) residency program in Hong Kong accepts crypto assets as valid documentation of assets. It enables crypto holders to meet the eligibility criteria. articles.title: Wall Street’s Ethereum Short Bets Explode – Should Investors Worry?
articles.description: In a dramatic shift, hedge funds appear to be ramping up short positions in Ethereum at a rate not seen before, sparking questions on whether the second‐largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization could be facing troubled waters—or if something else is at play. articles.title: Hong Kong Approves Bitcoin and Ethereum for Investment Immigration
articles.description: Hong Kong now accepts Bitcoin and Ethereum as proof of assets for investment immigration. Applicants must hold HKD 30 million in crypto. articles.title: University of Austin Invests in Bitcoin as Endowments Turn to Crypto
articles.description: The University of Austin is making major moves in institutional Bitcoin adoption, planning to launch a dedicated Bitcoin investment fund. This initiative reflects the increasing interest among US institutions to adopt Bitcoin and other digital assets.
